findings.title: Stale-link cleanup skips the legacy ~/bin/specflow symlink
findings.detail: FR-008 and the spec's edge-case example explicitly call out old links such as ~/bin/specflow, but the new cleanup loop only scans specflow-*. That pattern never matches specflow without a hyphen, so the main legacy link this feature is supposed to remove is left behind. Change the scan to cover specflow* while still guarding with [[ -L "$link" ]].

findings.title: Existing projects still can miss required prompt files after update
findings.detail: specflow-init --update still refreshes only ~/.claude/commands and never installs missing .specflow prompt files. Older projects that lack review_plan_prompt.txt or review_impl_rereview_prompt.txt will still fail when /specflow.plan or /specflow.fix tries to read them.

findings.title: No regression coverage for the new migration logic
findings.detail: The new behavior adds upgrade-path logic, but the diff includes no automated verification for stale-link cleanup or older-project prompt migration.

findings.title: Stale-link cleanup deletes unrelated ~/bin/specflow* symlinks
findings.detail: The new cleanup loop removes any symlink under ~/bin matching specflow* whenever this checkout lacks a same-named file in bin/. That will delete valid user-managed symlinks such as ~/bin/specflow-custom, or links from another specflow checkout, even when their targets still exist.
